model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Florida has the stronger overall two-way profile in the major efficiency ratings (KenPom #4; Torvik #4), and its elite defense (top-5 on both) is the most reliable separator in a Sweet 16 game against an opponent that can really score. Vanderbilt’s upset path is very real because its offense is elite (AdjO #6 on both systems) and it has already shown it can punish Florida when it forces turnovers and gets out in transition, but Florida’s higher defensive ceiling and slightly better overall margin give it more stable ways to win if it plays to form. The main swing factor is Florida’s lead-guard health/availability, so confidence stays moderate rather than high.

reasoningStep.title: Sweet 16 (South) — Florida vs Vanderbilt
reasoningStep.summary: Pick Florida based on a better overall efficiency margin and a notably stronger defense, while acknowledging Vanderbilt’s elite offense and prior success make this a live upset spot.
evidence: KenPom: Florida #4 (AdjEM 33.83; AdjD 91.8) vs Vanderbilt #12 (AdjEM 28.12; AdjD 99.2).
Torvik: Florida #4 (DE rank 5) vs Vanderbilt #10 (DE rank 28); Vanderbilt offense ranks #6 on both systems.
Head-to-head context: Vanderbilt beat Florida 91-74 in the SEC tournament (March 14, 2026), showing a credible upset path if it wins the turnover/pace battle.
Availability watch: ESPN reported Florida PG Walter Clayton Jr. previously sat with an ankle issue vs Vanderbilt, which could affect Florida’s offensive floor if it resurfaces.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
